AI-powered interview assistant

Getting Started

On first launch Career Copilot Premium walks you through a short setup wizard. You only need to complete this once — your answers are saved to your profile.

Home Screen

The Home screen is your launchpad. Choose the target role, company name, and AI model, then click Start Session to begin a live interview.

Sidebar

Click the sidebar toggle (top-left) to expand the navigation panel. From here you can jump to any section of the app or open the overlay window.

Linking Your Phone

Mirror live AI answers on your phone screen during the interview. This is useful when your laptop screen is shared with the interviewer.

  1. Open the sidebar and click Link Device.
  2. Scan the QR code with your phone camera, or type the 6-digit code shown below it.
  3. Your phone immediately starts showing the same answer cards as the desktop.

Starting a Session

After filling in the role and company on the Home screen, click Start Session. The app switches to the Live Session view and begins listening for audio (if you enabled Live Listen).

During a Live Interview

While a session is active the app continuously processes audio and surfaces relevant answer cards. You can guide the AI at any point using the steer input.

Steer Input

Type a hint or topic into the Steer field at the bottom of the Live Session view and press Enter. The AI will factor your hint into its next response.

Overlay

Press F3 at any time to show or hide the compact overlay window that floats above your other applications. You can also click Open Overlay in the sidebar. Closing the overlay hides it — press F3 again to bring it back.

Keyboard shortcuts

KeyAction
F2Toggle live audio listening
F3Show / hide the overlay window

Ending a Session

Click End Session in the header. The session is saved and you are returned to the Home screen.

Session History

The Sessions page lists every past interview session. Click any row to review the full transcript and AI answers for that session.

Your Profile

The Profile page shows the information collected during onboarding: your name, resume summary, and skill tags. You can update any field here at any time.

System Status

The System Status page shows the health of each subsystem — Flask server, audio device, AI model connectivity, and mobile bridge. Use this to troubleshoot any component that is not working as expected.

Settings

The Settings screen lets you update your API keys, change the active audio device, and adjust overlay and theme preferences.
